session_summary: A rigor pass that started with Marcus refusing to draft the manuscript until each decomposition term was shown unbiased, drove a multi-step verification chase (variance reduction, analytical Hessians, expected-vs-sample RIV, then the one-RIV resolution), and ended by catching the Term-A-under-MAR non-cancellation and its likely Shimodaira precedent. Marcus's role was the standard-setter and skeptic: he posed the organizing question, demanded reproducibility and in-document checks, insisted simulations match the derivation, drove literature acquisition, and made every scope/framing call while the AI ran the compute.

coded_turns.quote: We haven't shown in the manuscript for example that whether each of the three terms are unbaised, or whether  the remainder/error really follow an expected relationship with n.
coded_turns.note: Refuses to draft; names the missing evidence (per-term unbiasedness, remainder-vs-n) that sets the whole session's agenda.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/008 rigor pass; derivation.qmd expected-vs-sample RIV rewrite

coded_turns.quote: This all seemed to be a bit stream of consciousness and it was a bit hard to follow.
coded_turns.note: Rejects the AI's rambling output structure; asks it to reframe in problem-vs-solution terms.

coded_turns.quote: What do you mean variance reduction? How?
coded_turns.note: Probes the proposed variance-reduction method before accepting it.

coded_turns.quote: I like your suggestions
coded_turns.note: Approves the sample-RIV-primary + confirm-A-first plan after the explanation.

coded_turns.quote: Why numerical hessians? Aren’t analytical hessians easy enough?

coded_turns.quote: I like your suggestion
coded_turns.note: Approves option (a) brute-force pin of E[A],E[B],E[T].

coded_turns.quote: Can you see if we can derive it analytically and if we can put it as an appendix of the derivations guide? It may already be derived in matrix cookbook or other reference?
coded_turns.note: Directs an analytic derivation as an appendix and flags Matrix Cookbook as a likely source.

coded_turns.quote: I agree. We want both
coded_turns.note: Approves both Appendix C and the derivation-consistent W3 correction arm.

coded_turns.quote: Let’s go ahead and delete the current manuscript. It isn’t good and we’ll need to start from scratch with an outline I make
coded_turns.note: Scraps the existing manuscript and reserves the new outline for himself.

coded_turns.quote: Have things been smoke tested to make sure that there's no surprises?
coded_turns.note: Demands smoke-testing of the new two-information machinery before relying on it.

coded_turns.quote: So basically we just need to run the full battery again? Don’t do it. Just asking
coded_turns.note: Clarifies what a full refresh would require while explicitly withholding the go-ahead.

coded_turns.quote: how are we evaluating the rate of decay? Are we fitting to a grid of n values and actually fitting a regression
coded_turns.note: Probes whether the decay rate is actually fitted; exposes that the rate was asserted, not measured.

coded_turns.quote: Can't you randomly sample sample size values as part of your grid? And I'm not sure a uniform distribution for the random sampling is ideal with log log regression.
coded_turns.note: Contributes design judgment on the n-grid sampling and the log-log regression assumption.

coded_turns.quote: I’m fine with fixed log
coded_turns.note: Accepts log-spacing as the resolved design point.

coded_turns.quote: for each rep uniformly sample a sample size n in log(200), log(1E4) rather than say selecting a fixed grid of say 20 equi spaced values on that interval. Why is that not better?
coded_turns.note: Presses the AI to justify fixed grid over per-rep random log-uniform n; statistical design challenge.

coded_turns.quote: Ok sounds good,
coded_turns.note: Accepts the fixed-log-grid, average-per-N, WLS log-log design.

coded_turns.quote: Yes we should write the design down and then build it

ai_side.evidence: I lean: sample-RIV primary, confirm A first. Nothing touched in the manuscript or production code yet.
ai_side.note: Laid out sample-RIV-primary vs expected-info-primary with a recommendation.

ai_side.evidence: (a) Brute force. ... (b) Stronger variance reduction. ... I lean (a) now (pure compute, decisive)
ai_side.note: Two precision-pinning options with trade-offs and a lean.

ai_side.evidence: but it's your call on whether that question is worth 20 minutes now or whether you'd rather I start drafting. Which way?
ai_side.note: Put the run-vs-draft decision explicitly to Marcus.

ai_side.evidence: the manuscript can't keep hand-waving it as "passes."
ai_side.note: Flagged that the +1/2tr(RIV) headline is asymptotic and off at realistic n.

ai_side.evidence: the derivation needs to (1) retract the "residuals cancel" claim, (2) name the three informations
ai_side.note: Self-flagged that a prior derivation claim must be retracted.

ai_side.evidence: route 1 (Rubin, expensive, practical) or route 2 (analytic observed Hessian, cheaper, MVN-only)? I lean route 1
ai_side.note: Two estimator routes for tr_samp with trade-offs.

ai_side.evidence: This is the most consequential thing the rigor turned up.
ai_side.note: Flagged that the rate check may have surfaced an incomplete expected-info benchmark under MAR.

ai_side.evidence: E[T]=½tr(RIV) appears to need an O(1) Term-A correction under MAR that is material (~8%)
ai_side.note: Flagged a genuine finding against the headline theorem requiring a framing decision.

ai_side.evidence: this is now a reframing decision for both papers ... since it spans both papers. Want me to record it and hold?
ai_side.note: Put the cross-paper reframing decision to Marcus rather than deciding.

ai_side.evidence: (a) I make the source edits to w3-sweep.R now ... (b) Leave all code untouched ... (c) Do it all now ... My lean is (a)
ai_side.note: Three sequencing options for the code re-pointing with a lean.

proposed_codes.name: system-notification
proposed_codes.definition: A turn that is an automated task-completion or command-stdout notification injected into the user channel, carrying no human-authored move.
proposed_codes.example_quote: Background command "Run brute-force pin of E[A],E[B],E[T] in background" completed (exit code 0)

proposed_codes.name: withhold-go-ahead
proposed_codes.definition: The human asks a clarifying or scoping question while explicitly forbidding the AI from acting on it yet (information-only, no execution).
proposed_codes.example_quote: So basically we just need to run the full battery again? Don’t do it. Just asking
